question: Which alma maters have produced the highest number of NASA astronauts, and how does their representation vary across decades?

analysis.design_rationale: A heatmap visually encodes the number of astronauts from each top alma mater across decade bins, blending time and institutional representation. This approach reveals temporal shifts: which universities rose or declined in astronaut production in different eras. Audiences can see whether dominant schools have sustained influence or experienced peak periods, uncovering trends in educational backgrounds of astronauts over time.
analysis.target_insight: Shows how the representation of top alma maters among NASA astronauts varied across different decades.

chart_encodings.color: Astronaut count per alma per decade (heatmap cells)
chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Color intensity proportional to count; colorbar showing scale; decade labels

transformations: Drop rows where df['Alma Mater'] or df['Year'] is null
Group by df['Alma Mater'] and Decade, aggregate count of df['Name']
Identify top 10 alma maters by overall astronaut count
Pivot for heatmap: rows = alma maters, columns = decades, values = astronaut count
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
df = pd.read_csv("csv_c_squared\261.csv")
df = df.dropna(subset=['Alma Mater', 'Year'])
df['Decade'] = (df['Year']//10*10).astype(int).astype(str) + "s"
astronaut_counts = df.groupby(['Alma Mater','Decade'])['Name'].count().reset_index()
top10_alma = df.groupby('Alma Mater')['Name'].count().sort_values(ascending=False).head(10).index
astronaut_counts_top = astronaut_counts[astronaut_counts['Alma Mater'].isin(top10_alma)]
heatmap_data = astronaut_counts_top.pivot(index='Alma Mater', columns='Decade', values='Name').fillna(0)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10,6))
cmap = mpl.colormaps["viridis"]
im = ax.imshow(heatmap_data, aspect="auto", cmap=cmap)
ax.set_xticks(np.arange(len(heatmap_data.columns)))
ax.set_yticks(np.arange(len(heatmap_data.index)))
ax.set_xticklabels(list(heatmap_data.columns))
ax.set_yticklabels(list(heatmap_data.index))
for i in range(len(heatmap_data.index)):
    for j in range(len(heatmap_data.columns)):
        val = int(heatmap_data.iloc[i,j])
        ax.text(j, i, str(val), ha="center", va="center", color="white" if heatmap_data.iloc[i,j]>0.8*heatmap_data.values.max() else "black")
plt.title("NASA Astronauts per Top 10 Alma Maters by Decade")
cbar = fig.colorbar(im, ax=ax, orientation='vertical', label="Astronaut Count")
plt.xlabel("Decade of Selection")
plt.ylabel("Alma Mater")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
